.,.'13,,.1$
\rr\-
Donald A. Barrier
567 East Lassen Ave. Space 218
chico, California 95926
Phone: (916) 342-4510
Eair Political Practices Conmission
P.O. Box 807 
November 15, 1990
Sacramento, California 95804
Attention: Mr. Steve Rhodes
Dear Mr. Rhodes:
As a private and patrj.otic citizen I see our democratic elective process
as a wonderful legacy, a right fought for with the blood of our young
and brave since 1776. Voting for someone of our choice is a privilege
on].y now being extended to countries throughout Eastern Europe who have
suffered for so many years under the oppressive boot of
totalitarianifli.
I am & years of age. I spent the last of my teen years in the service
of our great and free country during l4lorld War II as a l\,larine in the
South Pacific and China. In China I san first hand the colrmunist
takeover which nerely changed one oppressor (.fapan ) for another perhaps
even worse, the l'Iao evil Conmunist reginre.
I take our elective proc-ess very seriously and I .rm very upset by modern
day methods which border on or are over the line on possible illega1
slander. During the last few elections, the last minute hate,hit pieces
are distributed and no one is villing to stand up and claim their
responsibility until forced to do so. This is the case in the Chico City
Council election. The enclosed news articles are pretty self explanatory
and the letters to the editor show just how others feel about the
undertranded nethods used by opposition groups. Bear in mind that for
every letter received there are probably thousands who feel the same but
do not take the time or effort to write.
Aren't there laws that IIEke people accountable for their actions
regarding these hate mailers? Don't the parties involved have to declare
by 1aw their contribution for postage and in-kind services on their
respective campaigin fund disclosures? Is this disreputable practice
Iegal and if so wtlat do we do to nake it i11egal?
We read after every election just how apathetic the eligible voting
public seems to be. With each election vorse th.an the last it is not
hard to understand why people that all candidates are crooks. When we do
get a fine honest man like Tbd Hubert to run for office, they are
subjected to the lies from the opposition. Hrelan nature being wlnt it
is, people aluays seem to remenber the bad said about a person whether
it is true or not and as long as people like Mc Ginnis, Dolan,
l"irlhoUand and the Tom llayden mchine are allowed to continue elections
by fraud and deceit, things will only get norse.


It is tine to put an end to the corrupt groups like Hayden's Canpaign
california who think that they are above our rnoral and 1ega1 1aws. Can
you inragine just hou many Republican voters were mislead by the phony
voting guide which showed in very sma1l print (hard ror us oId tirers to
read) at the bottdn which stated it was printed and distributed by a
supposed group cal1ed "Citizens For Republican values". this phony hit
piece possibly cost Senator Jim Nielsen the election. An election won in
this manner is a shallow victory for all of us.
If this disregard for honesty is allowed !o continue ue might as well
beccme what the rest of the world knows is vrong through trial and error
and is throwing over, conrn:nism and Lotalitarianism.
Please peruse the enclosed material and supply ne with sonle answers.
Sincerely

sections.question_synthetic: The writer inquires whether there are laws requiring accountability for negative campaign mailers, whether contributors must disclose funding for such materials in campaign disclosures, and whether such practices are legal or should be made illegal.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming citizen request/complaint letter, not an FPPC advice letter response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The document contains the citizen's concerns about campaign ethics and requests for legal guidance rather than FPPC's authoritative response.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The writer inquires whether there are laws requiring accountability for negative campaign mailers, whether contributors must disclose funding for such materials in campaign disclosures, and whether such practices are legal or should be made illegal.

embedding.summary: A citizen writes to the FPPC expressing concerns about negative campaign tactics used in a Chico City Council election, specifically questioning the legality of anonymous or unaccountable 'hate mailers' and requesting clarification on campaign finance disclosure requirements.
